title: recruitment and hiring for the position of Fleet Manager Starting June 27, 2025

communications.subject: Montana Freedom of Information Act Request: recruitment and hiring for the position of Fleet Manager Starting June 27, 2025
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Montana Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Related to the recruitment and hiring for the position of Fleet Manager Starting June 27, 2025
1.	All applications and submitted resumes.
2.	All interview scoring sheets, evaluation forms, ranking or scoring records, and evaluation notes used to assess candidates.
3.	The final hiring recommendation, including any memos, attachments, or supporting documentation.

Where applicable, please include records reflecting compliance with the City of Bozeman Ethics Policy, the City of Bozeman Employee Handbook, and any fair hiring or equal employment opportunity procedures or documentation used in this hiring process.
Preferred format: electronic copies (PDF or searchable format). If fees will apply, please provide an estimate before processing. I request a waiver or reduction of fees if permitted, because disclosure would serve the public interest in transparency of municipal hiring practices.
Redaction request: To protect personal privacy consistent with Montana law, please redact only the minimum personal information necessary (personal phone numbers, personal email addresses, home addresses, social security numbers, and other clearly private identifiers) and release the remainder of the records, including professional qualifications, work history, interview scores or rankings, and substantive hiring recommendation and rationale.
If any records or portions are withheld, please cite the specific statutory exemption under Mont. Code Ann. § 2-6-101 et seq. relied upon for each withholding and provide a description sufficient to identify the nature of withheld material

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John777

title: FOIL Request – Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – Town of East Fishkill

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: FOIL Request – Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – Town of East Fishkill
communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Under the provisions of the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL), N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 84–90, I hereby request access to and copies of records held by the Town of East Fishkill, Dutchess County, relating to the planning, proposal, procurement, authorization, or attempted deployment of Flock Safety surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems.

This request is made in the public interest to better understand the use, consideration, or implementation of surveillance technologies by local government entities, including decision-making processes, vendor relationships, and potential impacts on public privacy.

The purpose of this request is to determine the origin, authorization process, procurement activity, proposed locations, and communications surrounding any surveillance camera system, including identifying which individual, agency, or vendor first initiated discussions or planning for such a program within the Town of East Fishkill.

Timeframe: January 1, 2023 – Present, including any earlier records referencing or relating to Flock Safety, automated license plate readers, or surveillance cameras within the Town of East Fishkill.

Records Requested

For the purpose of this request, the records I seek include, but are not limited to the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements

All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, vendor agreements, procurement documents, or proposals involving the Town of East Fishkill and Flock Safety or any related entities concerning surveillance cameras, automated license plate readers, or related monitoring technology.

This includes draft contracts, executed agreements, amendments, pilot program agreements, trial program documentation, cancellation notices, procurement justifications, and contract termination records.

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system  
• the first communications between the Town and Flock Safety  
• vendor outreach to town officials  
• internal discussions about introducing the technology  
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors  

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Town Supervisor  
• Town Board members  
• Town of East Fishkill Police Department personnel  
• Town administrative staff  
• Town legal counsel  
• Highway or Building Department personnel  
• other municipal offices  

that discuss:

• Flock Safety  
• automated license plate readers  
• surveillance cameras  
• camera deployments  
• vendor procurement or negotiations  
• surveillance technology adoption  

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the Town of East Fishkill and:

• representatives of Flock Safety  
• technology vendors or consultants  
• Dutchess County officials  
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies  
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers  

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks  
• vendor proposals  
• demonstration scheduling emails  
• meeting invitations or call summaries  
• webinar invitations  
• follow-up communications  

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the Town of East Fishkill.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements  
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps  
• deployment plans  
• proposed installation locations or intersections  
• roadway monitoring points  
• entry or exit routes to the town  
• communications discussing site selection  
• vendor recommendations for placement  
• field surveys or site inspections  
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement  

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans  
• camera location maps  
• infrastructure assessments  
• equipment specifications  
• installation schedules  
• operational capability descriptions  
• technology architecture documents  

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices  
• payment records  
• purchase authorizations  
• budget allocations  
• grant applications  
• cost analyses  
• procurement justifications  
• financial approvals  

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from Town Board meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where surveillance camera programs or Flock Safety systems were discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to surveillance camera programs or procurement processes.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data  
• policies governing license plate reader searches  
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks  
• data retention policies  
• privacy or civil liberties assessments  

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information  
• CC and BCC fields  
• full email header routing data  
• timestamps for sending and receipt  
• message ID fields  
• attachment metadata  
• document creation dates  
• last modified dates  
• author fields  
• file path information where available  

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter  
Independent Investigative Journalist

title: Flock Safety Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Flock Safety Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) Invoices, Contracts, and Purchase orders related to the company Flock Safety or Flock Group (Flock).

2) I request internal training documents and policies and procedures for the use of Flock tools.

3) I request any sharing agreements signed with other agencies as well as The City's sharing agreement it requires other agencies to conform to.

4) I request the web address of The City's Flock transparency portal, a free feature of Flock OS.

5) Lastly, I request all available audit data from the Flock OS audit tools. This should include the organization audit, network audit, and sharing report.
    A. Organization Audit in Flock.
    The report should include data logged from the period of installation to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done by the City.

    B. Network Audit in Flock.
    The report should include data logged from the period of installation, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the City’s Flock network by any agency the City has shared with in the Flock System.

    C. Sharing report in Flock.
    The report should include flock networks shared with The City and networks The City is sharing its network with.

I request that files be supplied in their original electronic, machine readable format rather than being converted to any alternate formats.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

title: Complaint Request

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Complaint Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request made under the California Public Records Act, Government Code sections 7920.000 – 7931.000, for records in the possession or control of your agency.

Please provide all complaints made to the Santa Cruz Police Department against, related to or regarding Gabriella Noack, candidate for the Santa Cruz City Council District 6 June Primary, filed between March 1, 2026 and March 18, 2026.

Please keep in mind your agency’s obligations under the law require you to:

1. Respond within 10 calendar days.

2. Produce electronic records in the format in which you hold them.

3. Help me to make a focused and effective request by (1) identifying records and information that are responsive, (2) describing the information technology and physical location in which the records exist and (3) providing suggestions for overcoming any practical issues.

4. Segregate or redact any exempt information contained within a record so that the public information in the remainder of the record may be released.

5. Broadly construe all provisions that further the public’s right of access, and apply any limitations on access as narrowly as possible, per Article 1, Section 3(b)(2) of the California Constitution.

6.Provide a written justification for any denial of this request, in whole or in part, stating the specific exemptions from disclosure that you assert and the name and title or position of each person responsible for the denial.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Moore

title: Georgia Public Records Request – DeKalb County Sheriff – DeLozier Case

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Public Records Request – DeKalb County Sheriff – DeLozier Case
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To: Records Division, DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office 

Date: March 17, 2026

Re: Public Records Request – Administrative Records Concerning Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., I respectfully request access to administrative, clerical, and procedural records maintained by the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office concerning the handling of incidents or actions involving myself, Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

This request is limited to administrative records and does not seek private or sensitive information about any other individual.

Requested Records:
1.  Incident Reports / Case Files
Administrative logs, internal notes, and summaries documenting incidents involving me.
Assignment records indicating which personnel reviewed or processed these incidents.
References to contractors, vendors, or third parties assisting in administrative handling.

2.  911 / Dispatch Records
Call logs, CAD entries, or summaries of dispatch activity referencing incidents involving me.
Metadata indicating times of receipt, personnel assigned, and administrative handling.

3.  Internal Communications / Administrative Memos
Emails, memoranda, or notes documenting the administrative handling, processing, or review of incidents involving me.
Documentation of decisions to close, escalate, or otherwise manage cases from a clerical or administrative perspective.

4.  Investigator / Deputy Assignments
Records showing which deputies or personnel were assigned to administrative handling of incidents involving me.
References to contractors or vendors who assisted in administrative work.

5.  Administrative Policies and Procedures
Written policies, procedures, or guidance governing the processing, review, and maintenance of incident records or dispatch logs.
Training materials or internal documentation relevant to clerical handling of cases.

6.  Metadata and Audit Trails
Timestamps, system-generated logs, or audit trails documenting access, review, or updates to administrative records concerning me.

While this request concerns myself as the requester, it is limited strictly to administrative and procedural records. It is submitted in the public interest to document the Sheriff’s Office handling of incidents and clerical actions. This request does not seek investigative opinions, confidential officer notes, or privileged material, and is therefore not subject to Privacy Act or investigative exemptions. The public record created by this submission establishes a transparent timeline of accountability and ensures that routine administrative handling cannot be withheld under claims of ongoing investigation.

A corresponding request for full records, including sensitive identifiers and information necessary for administrative review, will be submitted directly to the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office via USPS. This public-facing submission documents transparency and accountability, while the USPS submission ensures that all legally available records relating to my interactions with the Office are fully identified and obtained.
 
This request is submitted to document the administrative handling of incidents involving myself and to ensure transparency and public accountability in the operations of the DeKalb County Sheriff’s Office.

Format and Fees:    Please provide records in electronic format if available. If any fees apply, please notify me in advance before processing.

Thank you for your attention and assistance in providing these public records.

Sincerely, 
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

title: FOIL Request – Surveillance Cameras / ALPR Systems (Flock Safety) – Town of Fishkill

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: FOIL Request – Surveillance Cameras / ALPR Systems (Flock Safety) – Town of Fishkill
communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Under the provisions of the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL), N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 84–90, I hereby request access to and copies of records held by the Town of Fishkill, Dutchess County, relating to the planning, proposal, procurement, authorization, or attempted deployment of Flock Safety surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems.

This request is made in the public interest to better understand the use, consideration, or implementation of surveillance technologies by local government entities, including decision-making processes, vendor relationships, and potential impacts on public privacy.

The purpose of this request is to determine the origin, authorization process, procurement activity, proposed locations, and communications surrounding any surveillance camera system, including identifying which individual, agency, or vendor first initiated discussions or planning for such a program within the Town of Fishkill.

Timeframe: January 1, 2023 – Present, including any earlier records referencing or relating to Flock Safety, automated license plate readers, or surveillance cameras within the Town of Fishkill.

Records Requested

For the purpose of this request, the records I seek include, but are not limited to the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements

All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, vendor agreements, procurement documents, or proposals involving the Town of Fishkill and Flock Safety or any related entities concerning surveillance cameras, automated license plate readers, or related monitoring technology.

This includes draft contracts, executed agreements, amendments, pilot program agreements, trial program documentation, cancellation notices, procurement justifications, and contract termination records.

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system  
• the first communications between the Town and Flock Safety  
• vendor outreach to town officials  
• internal discussions about introducing the technology  
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors  

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Town Supervisor  
• Town Board members  
• Town of Fishkill Police Department personnel  
• Town administrative staff  
• Town legal counsel  
• Highway or Building Department personnel  
• other municipal offices  

that discuss:

• Flock Safety  
• automated license plate readers  
• surveillance cameras  
• camera deployments  
• vendor procurement or negotiations  
• surveillance technology adoption  

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the Town of Fishkill and:

• representatives of Flock Safety  
• technology vendors or consultants  
• Dutchess County officials  
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies  
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers  

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks  
• vendor proposals  
• demonstration scheduling emails  
• meeting invitations or call summaries  
• webinar invitations  
• follow-up communications  

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the Town of Fishkill.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements  
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps  
• deployment plans  
• proposed installation locations or intersections  
• roadway monitoring points  
• entry or exit routes to the town  
• communications discussing site selection  
• vendor recommendations for placement  
• field surveys or site inspections  
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement  

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans  
• camera location maps  
• infrastructure assessments  
• equipment specifications  
• installation schedules  
• operational capability descriptions  
• technology architecture documents  

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices  
• payment records  
• purchase authorizations  
• budget allocations  
• grant applications  
• cost analyses  
• procurement justifications  
• financial approvals  

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from Town Board meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where surveillance camera programs or Flock Safety systems were discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to surveillance camera programs or procurement processes.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data  
• policies governing license plate reader searches  
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks  
• data retention policies  
• privacy or civil liberties assessments  

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information  
• CC and BCC fields  
• full email header routing data  
• timestamps for sending and receipt  
• message ID fields  
• attachment metadata  
• document creation dates  
• last modified dates  
• author fields  
• file path information where available  

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter  
Independent Investigative Journalist

title: FAA - UAS Testing in Columbia River Gorge

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FAA - UAS Testing in Columbia River Gorge
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Dear FOIA Officer,

Under the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I am requesting copies of the following records related to Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS) testing, certification, and operations within the geographic boundaries of the Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area and surrounding vicinities.

Geographic Scope:
This request specifically encompasses UAS operations within:

- Hood River County, OR

- Wasco County, OR (including The Dalles Municipal Airport - KDLS)

- Skamania County, WA

- Klickitat County, WA (specifically the vicinities of Bingen, Husum, and Roland Lake)

Requested Records:

Certificates of Waiver or Authorization (COAs): All issued COAs, including original applications, maps of proposed flight paths, and supporting documentation, granted to the following entities between January 1, 1998, and the present:

- Insitu, Inc.

- The Boeing Company (specifically regarding Insitu or Phantom Works UAS programs).

- Hood Technology Corporation (or Hood Tech).

- Aerovel Corporation.

- Cloud Cap Technology (a subsidiary of Collins Aerospace/UTC).

Special Airworthiness Certificates (SACs): All SACs in the "Experimental" category issued for UAS research, development, and market survey to the entities listed above within the specified geographic scope and timeframe.

Restricted or Prohibited Airspace Requests: Any correspondence or formal requests from the listed entities to establish Temporary Flight Restrictions (TFRs) or designate restricted/warning areas over the Columbia River Gorge for "sensor calibration," "acoustic testing," or "system validation."

Incident & Safety Reports: Any records of UAS "mishaps," near-misses, unauthorized incursions, or property damage reports involving aircraft operated by the listed entities within the Portland and Seattle Flight Standards District Office (FSDO) jurisdictions.

Format of Records:
I request these records be provided in an electronic format (searchable PDF or digital data files).

Fee Waiver & Public Interest:
I am requesting a fee waiver as the disclosure of this information is in the public interest. I am a journalist and researcher investigating the historical and modern integration of military-grade surveillance technology into civilian and rural American airspace. This research explores the social, environmental, and privacy impacts of aerospace development within a Federally Protected National Scenic Area. The information retrieved will contribute significantly to public understanding of how "dual-use" technologies (tuna tracking vs. military surveillance) are tested and deployed in domestic environments.

Thank you for your assistance with this request. I look forward to receiving your determination within the 20-business-day statutory limit.

Sincerely,

Katherine Bertash
Editor, The Skamania Dispatch

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kate Bertash

City of Sausalito ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-25 has been submitted successfully. Thank you for your request. Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, Government Code Section 7920.000, et seq., (the “Act”), this is to confirm receipt of your request for records you sent to the City of Sausalito (“The City”).   Under Government Code 7922.535, the City has 10-days from the receipt of the request, to make a determination as to whether it possesses responsive records, unless there is a basis for an extension. You may email CityClerk@sausalito.gov if you have not received any response from the City after 10 days of submitting your request. Please note that the Public Records Act, the City is under no obligation to create or produce records that do not already exist at the time of request. The City will not produce records that are not subject to disclosure under the Public Records Act, and may redact records to protect non-disclosable information. If the City determines it is in possession of disclosable public records, the records will be provided electronically free of charge. Request for paper copies of releasable public record must be accompanied by fees to cover copying cost at $0.25 per page. ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact City of Sausalito.

title: Public Records Request – February 21, 2022 DUI Arrest of Stephanie Bloodworth

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request access to and copies of all records related to the February 21, 2022, DUI arrest of Stephanie Bloodworth on Duck Key, Monroe County, Florida. Bloodworth, 24, crashed her Tesla on her father’s private island while allegedly driving under the influence. Responding Monroe County deputies encountered her inside the wrecked vehicle, where she initially refused to exit, claiming, “My father owns this island!” Upon being removed, she resisted arrest, kicked and screamed, and was recorded on bodycam crying, “I need my dad here!” Deputies later used an airboat to recover evidence, including open vodka bottles. She was arrested and charged with DUI, resisting arrest, and fleeing law enforcement.

I am requesting the following records related to this incident:

All available body-worn camera footage from all deputies involved in the response, arrest, and transportation of Stephanie Bloodworth, specifically between 9:00 PM and 9:30 PM
All available dashboard camera dashcam footage from all responding patrol vehicles present at the scene
All 911 call recordings and dispatch logs related to this incident, including radio communications and CAD reports
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives, supplemental reports, and use-of-force documentation
The full DUI report, blood alcohol test results, and any toxicology findings associated with this case
All booking documents, jail intake records, and any recorded statements made by Stephanie Bloodworth following her arrest
All photographs taken by the Monroe County Sheriff's Office MCSO during or after the arrest, including images of the suspect, the crashed Tesla, the crash site, and recovered evidence such as open vodka bottles
Any video footage from airboat operations or any surveillance footage that may have captured the crash, response, or evidence recovery
Any internal reviews or reports related to this incident, including documents regarding the use of an airboat for evidence retrieval
Any press releases, social media posts, or official statements made by the Monroe County Sheriff’s Office regarding this incident

The primary goal of this request is to serve the public interest by making these records publicly accessible. While I acknowledge that I may also generate revenue from the dissemination of these materials, the request primarily serves to enhance transparency regarding this law enforcement incident.

I request a waiver of fees associated with fulfilling this request, as disclosure of the requested information will contribute to public understanding of law enforcement practices and actions. However, if a full fee waiver is not possible, please inform me of the estimated costs in advance. Additionally, if fees can be reduced or eliminated by excluding certain records, please specify which records those would be.

Please provide the records in an electronic format if available. If any portions of this request are denied, please cite the specific legal exemption(s) and provide a redacted version of the document where applicable.

I appreciate your time and assistance in this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Sam Nkata

To Whom It May Concern,

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act (NRS Chapter 239), I am requesting access to the following public records:

All body-worn camera (BWC) footage, dashcam footage, and any other audio or video recordings related to the following incident:

Case/Incident: Deobra Redden — Man Vaults Over Judge's Bench and Attacks Her Mid-Sentencing
Date of Incident: January 2, 2024
Agency: Clark County District Court
Summary: Las Vegas, Jan 3 2024. Deobra Redden, 30, being sentenced for felony battery. As Judge Mary Kay Holthus reads his criminal record, he vaults over the defense table and jumps the judge's bench, landing...


Note: I understand this footage has already been released to media outlets. I am requesting the same footage be provided directly to me as a member of the public under the applicable records law.

This request includes:
1. All body-worn camera footage from any officers involved in the incident
2. All dashcam / in-car camera footage from responding vehicles
3. Any booking room, interview room, or holding area footage related to this incident
4. Any associated arrest reports or incident reports (if not exempt)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: California Governor's Office of Business and Economic Development: Administrative Correspondence Regarding Atomic Machines, Inc.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting the following public records held by the Governor's Office of Business and Economic Development (GO-Biz):

Records Requested:
All correspondence, including but not limited to emails, email attachments, calendar invitations, meeting minutes, and text messages, between any staff members of GO-Biz and the following representatives of Atomic Machines, Inc.: 

Jeff Holden (CEO)
Klaus Zietlow (CTO)
Anjana Balakumar (Finance)
Tylee Holden (Growth)
Stephen Drinan (Go-to-Market)
Brandon Connors or Kevin Mukai (Manufacturing/Operations)
Erina DuBois (Executive Assistant to the CEO)

Search Parameters:

Date Range: January 1, 2024, to March 16th 2026

Please include a search for any emails sent to or from the @atomicmachines.com domain.

Keywords: Please include (but do not limit the search to) records containing the following terms: "incentives," "site selection," "expansion," "hiring commitments," "milestones,", "Jeff Holden", "Holden," "Atomic," or "Emeryville."

Legal Context & Segregability:
I am aware of the previous response from your office (dated March 16, 2026) citing Revenue and Taxation Code § 19542. Please note that this request is not seeking tax returns or proprietary financial data required to be filed for tax purposes. Rather, it seeks administrative communications regarding the coordination and status of the project.

Pursuant to Government Code § 7922.525, if any portion of these records is deemed exempt under RTC § 19542 or any other statute, you are required to redact the exempt information and provide the "reasonably segregable portion" of the records. A blanket denial of all correspondence based on a tax-return exemption is inconsistent with the transparency requirements of the CPRA. If certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Considering the implications of this technology, and the danger in hiding the financing and support of these types of companies from the public, there is a public interest in the presumption of transparency. Thank you, and I look forward to working together in resolving this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware
